# Primary sources

Recheck target and workspace behavior against the selected Cargo version at execution time. When
network access is unavailable, report the freshness gap.

- [Cargo project layout](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/guide/project-layout.html) — conventional package directories and targets.
- [Cargo targets](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/cargo-targets.html) — library, binary, example, test, and benchmark target discovery/configuration.
- [Cargo workspaces](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/workspaces.html) — members, resolver, inheritance, lockfile, target directory, and root-only tables.
- [Cargo features](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/features.html) — additive feature behavior and optional dependency implications.
- [The Rust Reference: modules](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/items/modules.html) — module source filenames, visibility, and organization.
- [The Rust Book: packages, crates, and modules](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch07-00-managing-growing-projects-with-packages-crates-and-modules.html) — language-level organization model.
- [The Rust Book: integration tests](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch11-03-test-organization.html) — unit versus integration test boundaries.
- [The Rust API Guidelines](https://rust-lang.github.io/api-guidelines/) — non-mandatory public API design considerations.
- [Agent Skills specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) — portable skill structure and progressive disclosure.
- [Codex skills](https://developers.openai.com/codex/build-skills) — current Codex discovery and authoring guidance.
- [Claude Code skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) — current Claude Code skill behavior and format.
- [OpenCode skills](https://opencode.ai/docs/skills/) — current OpenCode skill discovery and compatibility guidance.
